context:
Overview of Employment Sponsorship, Visa
Process & Billing Structure
1. Employment & Visa Structure
I am legally employed under an employment sponsorship arrangement with Nathan & Nathan
(Dynamic Employment Services L.L.C). My residence visa, Emirates ID, and medical insurance are
all issued under this sponsorship. All attached documents relate to this compliant employment
structure.
2. Payroll Record (Direct Debit / Direct Credit – Compliance Mechanism)
• For payroll and employment compliance purposes, a notional monthly salary of AED 1,600 is
recorded.
• As I am not paid a traditional salary by the company, this amount is temporarily debited from my
bank account each month.
• On the company’s payroll date, the same amount (AED 1,600) is credited back to my bank
account.
• This direct debit and direct credit mechanism is used solely to reflect a salary entry for banking,
payroll, and visa compliance.
• The AED 1,600 does not represent a fee, cost, or deduction and has no net financial

eflect a salary entry for banking,
payroll, and visa compliance.
• The AED 1,600 does not represent a fee, cost, or deduction and has no net financial impact
on me.
3. Invoicing & Client Registration
Invoices are generated through an official invoicing portal. Clients are registered on the platform
and invoices are issued only to registered clients in compliance with UAE regulations.
4. Client Payment Process
Clients do not pay me directly. Invoice payments are made to the company account via the official
invoicing portal.
5. Transfer of Funds to Me (Service Provider)
After receipt of client funds, the company transfers the relevant amounts to my personal account,
ensuring transparent accounting and regulatory compliance.
6. VAT & Service Fee
VAT (5%) is charged to the client. A service fee of 2.5% is deducted from my earnings and is not
charged to the client.
7. Example
Service Amount: AED 10,000
VAT (5%): AED 500
Total Paid by Client: AED 10,500
Service Fee (2.5%): AED 250
Net Amount

charged to the client.
7. Example
Service Amount: AED 10,000
VAT (5%): AED 500
Total Paid by Client: AED 10,500
Service Fee (2.5%): AED 250
Net Amount Received by Me: AED 9,750
8. Payment Flow Summary
Client → Invoicing Portal → Company Account → My Personal Account
Health Insurance Coverage Overview
Insurance Provider & Plan
Medical insurance is provided under the employment sponsorship with Dynamic Employment
Services L.L.C through an approved UAE insurer (SALAMA / NAS), Category F, compliant with
DHA requirements.
Geographical Coverage
• United Arab Emirates (UAE)
• Declared Home Country (as per policy terms)
Annual Coverage Limit
• Overall annual medical coverage limit: AED 160,000
In-Patient Benefits
• Hospital accommodation (shared / semi-private room)
• Surgical procedures and anesthesia
• Physician, surgeon, and specialist fees
• Intensive care and emergency treatment
• Prescribed medicines and hospital materials
Out-Patient Benefits
• General practitioner and specialist consul

Intensive care and emergency treatment
• Prescribed medicines and hospital materials
Out-Patient Benefits
• General practitioner and specialist consultations (subject to co-payment)
• Diagnostic tests and radiology (MRI/CT subject to approval)
• Prescribed pharmaceuticals (within annual limits)
• Physiotherapy (limited sessions with approval)
Emergency & Additional Benefits
• Emergency treatment and ambulance services within the UAE
• Day-care and outpatient surgical procedures
• Teleconsultation services (where applicable)
Maternity & Preventive Care (If Applicable)
• Maternity-related outpatient and inpatient coverage (subject to policy limits)
• Preventive screenings and essential vaccinations as per DHA guidelines
Regulatory Compliance
This insurance policy meets the minimum health insurance requirements mandated by the Dubai
Health Authority (DHA) for UAE residence visa holders.

DExclusions (please refer DHA Exclusion)
* Rates assume that the scheme is compulsory for all eligible members.
* Rates assume that all eligible members are actively at work on their normal inclusion date. If this is not the case, 
the member cannot join the scheme until the actively at work condition is met. 
* All the insured members should have valid residence visa of UAE
* All healthcare services for internationally and locally recognized epidemics are not covered
* It is mandatory to have coverage under HAAD compliant product if any/all of the members holding Abu Dhabi Visa
 And this quotation is not valid for the members who are Holding Abu Dhabi visa
* Only lives currently aged 65 years or less are eligible.
* Additions and Deletions will be on pro rata basis.
* Children are covered from birth (to be informed withi

ently aged 65 years or less are eligible.
* Additions and Deletions will be on pro rata basis.
* Children are covered from birth (to be informed within 1st 7 days after birth), Applicable age for child dependents is 0 to 18 years.
* Dependents include only spouses and children (0-18). Other relations such as, parents, brothers, sisters etc., 
are not eligible for cover as dependents.
* Errors & Omissions are excluded
* We reserve the right to revise the quotation in case the membership and claim statistics varies. 
*for NE members - Exclusions as per DHA Regulatory
***  (1) The above quoted rates are excluding VAT 
*** (2) As instructed by DHA, an additional premium of AED 19/- for BASMAH initiative and AED 18/- for HCV program (Total of AED 37/-) will be charged for Dubai Visa holding members. T
his amount will be subject to VAT and this amount is not eligible for refund. 
The amount collected by the insurance company will be maintained under a totally separate fund and account as adv

amount is not eligible for refund. 
The amount collected by the insurance company will be maintained under a totally separate fund and account as advised by DHA
*** (3) As instructed by Federal Authority, an additional premium of AED 24/- for Slash Data will be charged for Non Dubai Visa Holders . 
This amount will be subject to VAT and this amount is not eligible for refund. 
The amount collected by the insurance company will be maintained under a totally separate fund and account as advised by Federal Authority

However, in case of late addition Medical Application Form will be applicable.

he Medical Application Form.

Moreover, If the number of members above the age bracket of 65 & above increases over 2.5% of the total population of the group, the requirement of MAF will prevail.
3)All re-imbursement conditions must be fulfilled; missed or incomplete documents will not be accepted.
4)Please note that in case the benefits fall below the minimum required by DHA or there is a benefit which is not provided in this TOB but is required by DHA, then the cover under the policy will 
automatically increase/include the benefit to the same level as required by DHA.

Bank, in consideration of the Creditor accepting payments through the UAEDDS, the Creditor Bank 
implementing the requests for payments and ourselves for making payments to the Creditor through the 
UAEDDS.
2. The terms of this Mandate also incorporate (and you are bound by) the Rules of the UAEDDS ("Rules") which 
are available at www.centralbank.ae and any terms and conditions governing our relationship with you. If 
you fail to comply with your obligations under this Mandate, the Rules or our terms and conditions, this may

overning our relationship with you. If

3. Please note that the Rules refer to you as the 'Payer', us as the 'Paying Bank', the Creditor as the 
'Originator' and the Creditor's Bank as the 'Sponsoring Bank'. In the Rules, the Mandate is called, variously,
the 'Direct Debit Authority' or 'DDA.            Therefore, for the purposes of the Rules and this Mandate, those 
terms should be understood accordingly.
4. You are obliged to maintain sufficient funds in your account in order to meet the payments specified in this 
Mandate. Partial settlement of direct debit claims, save in limited circumstances, will not be executed.
5. We will act upon your written request to cancel this Mandate, or request that particular

aims, save in limited circumstances, will not be executed.
5. We will act upon your written request to cancel this Mandate, or request that particular payments not be 
made (termed a 'Stop Request') at your own discretion.  In the absence of such requests, we will continue to
act on the basis of the Mandate in place at that time.  Your written requests to amend or cancel this 
Mandate, or request that particular payments not be made can also be submitted through the Originator.    
It may take up to 5 working days to Cancel/Amend the Mandate.  In the meantime direct debits will 
continue as normal.
6. Where a payment request is lodged by the Creditor's Bank into the UAEDDS and it is rejected for any 
reason, the Creditor Bank is entitled to re-present the payment request up to a maximum of four times.  We
will charge you for every payment request and represented request that are being returned due to 
insufficient funds in your account.
7. If you believe that the payment out of your ac

request and represented request that are being returned due to 
insufficient funds in your account.
7. If you believe that the payment out of your account by direct debit should not have been made, then you 
should inform us in writing within 30 (thirty) days of the statement date of your account showing the 
payment, then in the absence of us agreeing otherwise, we are not obliged to entertain any request to 
refund your account with the relevant amount. The basis upon which we are obliged to provide a refund are
limited, and relate to the application of the Rules and, for the avoidance of doubt, do not include issues in 
connection with your contract or arrangements with the Creditor and are set out in the Rules.
8. It is your obligation to exercise reasonable care and vigilance in the operation of your account in the 
context of direct debit payments. We will make payments on the basis of this Mandate (as from time to time
amended validly), and are not otherwise obliged to review o

t debit payments. We will make payments on the basis of this Mandate (as from time to time
amended validly), and are not otherwise obliged to review or inform you about activity on your account, 
unless mutually agreed and except as required by the Rules.
9. You are not permitted to close the account to which this Mandate relates without making arrangements with
the Creditor to do so, and validly cancelling the Mandate in accordance with these terms.
10. If you request a refund and we turn down your request, then, if you remain dissatisfied, you should either 
try to resolve the matter with the Creditor or you may request the UAE Central Bank to consider the issue 
using the UAEDDS Dispute Resolution Procedure. The UAE Central Bank will not accept requests that are 
based upon your contract or arrangements with the Creditor, and generally has discretion as to whether it 
is prepared to intervene.
11. We may in certain circumstances, for example, in order to comply with relevant sanctio

has discretion as to whether it 
is prepared to intervene.
11. We may in certain circumstances, for example, in order to comply with relevant sanctions as imposed by 
CBUAE or anti-money laundering rules, refuse to follow a direct debit instruction or amend or cancel it (even
if the request is valid otherwise). We may also ask you for further information before executing a direct 
debit if we feel it necessary, and this may delay execution of your instruction.
12. If there is more than one direct debit on your account, the Direct Debit Requests from the Originator will be 
processed on a first-in first-out basis.  This will result in one or more Direct Debit Requests being 
dishonored if there are insufficient funds in your account to meet all payments.
13. Please note that if you choose a variable amount and decide not to agree a cap with your Creditor in this 
form, the Creditor will have an unlimited right to choose the amount deducted from your account, and the 
Paying Bank will h

th your Creditor in this 
form, the Creditor will have an unlimited right to choose the amount deducted from your account, and the

amount, unless the cap has been breached.
14. If the date(s) intimated by the Originator on which the Direct Debit Requests (DDR) under this Mandate will 
be raised falls on a holiday(s), then such DDR’s will be raised by the Originator within 7 business days 
immediately following the holiday(s).
15. Notices
15.a. Any notice relating to this Mandate shall be in writing and provided or accepted either in Arabic or 
in English or both.
15.b. Notices will be sent to you by pre-agreed means.
15.c. If a notice is received outside business hours, then it is received the next business day.
